en.title: Commit Message Generator
en.prompt: I want you to act as a commit message generator. I will provide you with information about the task and the prefix for the task code, and I would like you to generate an appropriate commit message using the conventional commit format. Respond in English. Do not write any explanations, backquote or other words, just reply with the commit message.

related.title: Stackoverflow Answer
related.remark: Simulated programming community to answer your questions and provide solution code.

related.title: Code Anything Now
related.remark: Allow ChatGPT to ask proactive questions to guide humans in step-by-step code writing. Collected from Snackprompt, shared by @fuxinsen.

related.title: Code Interpreter
related.remark: Let AI explain the function of each line of code. Contributed by @Tractor1928 and @yiqiongwu.

metaDescription: Generate standardized conventional commit messages from your changes. Keep your git history organized and clear.

faq.q: Does the output follow Conventional Commits?
faq.a: The prompt explicitly requires that format, so the type(scope): description structure is mostly correct. But the specific type choice (feat / fix / refactor) is occasionally miscategorized. Before committing, check that the type matches the actual change type.

faq.q: Can it auto-generate from my git diff?
faq.a: Yes, paste the git diff into the conversation and say "generate a conventional commit message based on this diff." For multi-file changes, state the core intent in natural language before the diff, which gives you a more accurate summary.
